test.name: Reclassify death-cert birth fact reported by son-in-law
test.description: Assertion a_012 captures Patrick's birth year as reported on his 1908 death certificate, where the informant was his son-in-law James Brown. James was not present at Patrick's birth in ~1845 — he's a secondary informant for that fact. The skill should recognize this and ensure the three classification layers reflect the secondary-informant reality, not the original-source surface.

input.user_message: Review the classification on a_012 — the death certificate birth fact for Patrick Flynn.

judge_context: Should explain why source_classification stays 'original' even though information_quality is secondary — the three layers are independent (the 1908 death certificate is itself an original source; the informant being distant from the event affects information quality, not source classification)

Should explain why the son-in-law is a secondary informant for Patrick's birth (James Brown was not present at his father-in-law's birth six decades earlier) — citing the relationship and the temporal gap

Should populate `informant_bias_notes` flagging that Brown may have confused Patrick's place of residence with his place of birth (the death cert says Pennsylvania, but the census records say Ireland — the conflict resolution in c_001 favored Ireland)
